description: You are a monitoring specialist who has just logged in to a fresh Linux workstation. Your first job is to prepare a very small, proof-of-concept alert environment that later engineers will extend. Perform everything from a regular, non-root shell in /home/user. Required final state (this is what the automated grader will check): 1. Directory • A directory named /home/user/monitoring must exist. 2. Persistent environment variable • The file /home/user/.bashrc must contain the exact line export ALERT_EMAIL=ops@example.com Do not add quotes or comments. • The line can appear anywhere in the file but must be on its own line (i.e., immediately followed by a newline). 3. Alert-rule configuration file • Create the file /home/user/monitoring/alert_rules.cfg. • The file must contain exactly one line: cpu>85%=>EMAIL • That line must be terminated with a single trailing newline character (\n). • No additional blank lines or whitespace are allowed before or after that line. 4. Setup log • Create a log file named /home/user/monitoring/setup.log. • The file must contain exactly the three lines shown below, each terminated with \n and no extra spaces or blank lines: START RULES_CONFIGURED ENV_VAR_ADDED Important notes and constraints: • You may use any standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities, editors, or redirection techniques, but keep the solution to no more than 2–3 shell commands in total. • Do not request or require elevated privileges (no sudo). • The grader will fail if any of the four items above deviate in spelling, ordering or newline placement. • Make sure that the files and directory are owned by the current user and have default permissions (the agent's umask is fine). Once finished, the system should have the directory, the updated .bashrc, the single-line rule file, and the three-line log file exactly as specified. The grader will read them directly to verify correctness.
truth:
Expected filesystem state after successful completion:

1. Directory:
/home/user/monitoring  — must exist.

2. File:
/home/user/.bashrc
   • Must contain the line:
     export ALERT_EMAIL=ops@example.com\n
   (Line may sit anywhere in file.)

3. File:
/home/user/monitoring/alert_rules.cfg
   • Exact contents (including final newline):
     cpu>85%=>EMAIL\n

4. File:
/home/user/monitoring/setup.log
   • Exact contents (including newlines):
     START\n
     RULES_CONFIGURED\n
     ENV_VAR_ADDED\n
